input.description: Read rebase-placeholder DInD to DOOD PR context
Full commit message for dind to dood

content.text:
## Root cause analysis: Karma `ChromeHeadless` sandbox failure

### What failed

`ng test --browsers=ChromeHeadless` (via `precommit-check` → `test:unit`) in `/workspaces/rewrite-ab-d1439f/ui`. Karma finds and launches Chromium, then Chromium exits immediately:

```
No usable sandbox! … install the chromium-sandbox package … or try --no-sandbox
```

This is **not** “Chrome not found.” The binary at `CHROME_BIN=/usr/bin/chromium` is present and invoked; the **Linux sandbox layer** cannot initialize inside the DevPod container.

---

### Architecture (how the pieces connect)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[generic-devpod-image ECR] -->|Stage 1: devcontainer build| B[javascript-node:22-trixie + features]
  B -->|Stage 2: Dockerfile.embed| C[Image with apt chromium, tmux, ttyd, ngrok]
  C --> D[DevPod workspace REBASE_DEVPOD_WORKSPACE_IMAGE]
  D --> E[Clone e.g. rewrite-ab-d1439f from rebase-placeholder template]
  E --> F[npm run precommit-check → Karma ChromeHeadless]
  F --> G["/usr/bin/chromium (system package)"]
```

- **Base image**: `mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/javascript-node:22-trixie` (Debian Trixie)
- **Published image**: `public.ecr.aws/k1t8c0v2/devpod-generic` (built from [generic-devpod-image](https://github.com/trilogy-group/generic-devpod-image))
- **App under test**: Angular UI from [rebase-placeholder](https://github.com/trilogy-group/rebase-placeholder) template
- **Karma config**: unchanged since initial import — uses system Chrome via `CHROME_BIN`, **no** `--no-sandbox` or custom launcher flags

---

### Primary root cause: system Chromium in a non-privileged container without sandbox workarounds

Karma uses **Debian’s system Chromium** (`/usr/bin/chromium`), not Playwright’s bundled browser. System Chromium expects a working sandbox (user namespaces and/or a setuid sandbox helper). Standard DevPod/Docker containers run **unprivileged**, which typically blocks that.

The app repo never configured Karma for container use:

- `ui/karma.conf.cjs` — sets `CHROME_BIN` fallback paths only; **no** `customLaunchers` with `--no-sandbox` / `--disable-dev-shm-usage`
- `ui/package.json` — `test:unit` passes `--browsers=ChromeHeadless` only
- `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` — sets `CHROME_BIN=/usr/bin/chromium` but no Chrome flags

By contrast, Playwright in the same environment is fine. `scripts/ensure-playwright-browsers.sh` installs Playwright’s **own** Chromium bundle and verifies launch with Playwright’s API, which adds container-safe flags automatically. That’s why post-create can report Playwright OK while Karma still fails.

---

### Likely regression trigger: DInD → DOOD (2026-06-01)

Both repos switched Docker feature on the same day:

| Repo | Commit | Date | Change |
|------|--------|------|--------|
| [generic-devpod-image](https://github.com/trilogy-group/generic-devpod-image) | `c8ecf80` | 2026-06-01 | `docker-in-docker:2` → `docker-outside-of-docker:1` |
| [rebase-placeholder](https://github.com/trilogy-group/rebase-placeholder) | `783890d` (#18) | 2026-06-01 | Same change |

**Why this matters for Chrome:**

- **Docker-in-Docker** requires the devcontainer to run **`--privileged`**, which gives broader kernel capabilities and often lets Chromium’s sandbox initialize.
- **Docker-outside-of-Docker** does **not** require privileged mode — the container is more locked down.

The ECR image was rebuilt and published after this change (`c8ecf80` CI run succeeded 2026-06-01). Your failure on **2026-06-03** aligns with workspaces picking up the new image digest.

This is the strongest explanation for a **recent** regression. The Karma/sandbox gap was latent from day one (April 29 initial import); DInD’s privileged mode likely masked it until June 1.

---

### Secondary factor: recent Chromium churn in embed image (2026-05-31)

Relevant commits in `generic-devpod-image`:

| Commit | What happened |
|--------|----------------|
| `4a27b1c` (May 31) | ngrok/ttyd refactor **removed** `chromium` from `Dockerfile.embed` apt install |
| `3617d88` (May 31, ~1h later) | Restored `chromium` + build-time assert `test -x /usr/bin/chromium` |

If a workspace was pinned to the **broken** digest from `4a27b1c`, tests would fail differently (missing binary, or post-create `apt-get install chromium` fallback). Your logs show sandbox failure, so **Chromium is present** — consistent with the restored image (`3617d88`+) or rebase-placeholder’s post-create apt install.

That May 31 window is a separate transient failure mode, not the one in your log.

---

### What is **not** the root cause

| Hypothesis | Verdict |
|------------|---------|
| Chromium missing from image | **Ruled out** — launcher starts Chrome; error is sandbox-specific |
| Wrong browser path | **Ruled out** — `CHROME_BIN=/usr/bin/chromium` is set in devcontainer |
| Angular/Karma version bug | **Unlikely** — config unchanged since April 29 |
| rebase-placeholder code change | **Unlikely** — no karma/devcontainer changes since rebrand (May 11); only DOOD swap (June 1) |

---

### Timeline summary

```
2026-04-29  Both repos: trixie base + DInD + CHROME_BIN=/usr/bin/chromium, no --no-sandbox
2026-04-29  generic-devpod-image: chromium baked into Dockerfile.embed
2026-05-31  Chromium accidentally dropped (4a27b1c), restored (3617d88) — ~1h outage
2026-06-01  DInD → DOOD in both repos — removes privileged mode from runtime
2026-06-01  New ECR image published (includes DOOD + restored chromium)
2026-06-03  Karma ChromeHeadless fails: "No usable sandbox!"  ← your error
```

---

### Conclusion

**Root cause:** Karma unit tests launch **system Chromium** in an **unprivileged DevPod container** without sandbox-compatible configuration (`--no-sandbox`, custom seccomp, `chromium-sandbox` setuid setup, or privileged mode). The configuration gap existed since initial import; the **DInD → DOOD change on 2026-06-01** is the most plausible trigger for it surfacing now by removing the privileged runtime that previously allowed Chrome’s sandbox to work.

**Contributing factors:**
1. May 31 embed refactor briefly removed baked-in Chromium (different failure mode).
2. Playwright and Karma use different Chrome binaries/launch paths — Playwright can pass while Karma fails.
3. Post-create only checks `chromium --version`, which does not exercise the sandbox.

I did not change anything per your request. If you want a fix direction later, the usual options are Karma `customLaunchers` with `--no-sandbox`, point Karma at Playwright’s bundled Chromium, or restore a runtime that supports sandbox (privileged / userns / setuid helper) — each with different security tradeoffs.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
what fix would you recommend? I dont' want to do dind
</user_query>

Given you’re staying on **DOOD** (no DInD, no privileged mode), the right fix is to stop expecting system Chromium’s sandbox to work in the DevPod container. Configure Karma for a container-safe launch instead.

## Recommended fix: Karma `customLaunchers` with `--no-sandbox`

**Where:** [rebase-placeholder](https://github.com/trilogy-group/rebase-placeholder) — `ui/karma.conf.cjs` and `ui/package.json` (that’s the template cloned into workspaces like `rewrite-ab-d1439f`).

**Why this one:**
- Standard pattern for headless Chrome in Docker/DevContainers
- Small, localized change; no image or DInD changes
- Keeps using apt `chromium` already baked in `generic-devpod-image`
- Acceptable for **unit tests** in an isolated dev workspace (not browsing untrusted sites)
- Playwright already works because it launches with container-safe flags; Karma does not unless you add them

**What to add** in `karma.conf.cjs`:

```javascript
customLaunchers: {
  ChromeHeadlessCI: {
    base: 'ChromeHeadless',
    flags: [
      '--no-sandbox',
      '--disable-dev-shm-usage',
      '--disable-gpu',
    ],
  },
},
```

**Update** `test:unit` in `ui/package.json`:

```json
"test:unit": "ng test --watch=false --browsers=ChromeHeadlessCI --code-coverage"
```

`--disable-dev-shm-usage` avoids shared-memory issues in Docker; `--disable-gpu` avoids harmless GPU noise in headless CI.

You don’t need to change `generic-devpod-image` for this — Chromium is installed correctly; the failure is launch flags, not a missing binary.

---

## Alternatives (weaker or heavier)

| Option | Verdict |
|--------|---------|
| **Revert to DInD** | You ruled this out — and it’s the wrong lever for Karma anyway. |
| **Install `chromium-sandbox` / setuid helper** | Unreliable in unprivileged containers; often still fails without extra caps. |
| **Point Karma at Playwright’s bundled Chromium** | Works in theory, but couples unit tests to Playwright install order and a cache path; more fragile than flags on system Chrome. |
| **Run container `--privileged`** | Would avoid DInD but is a broad security regression for the whole workspace — worse than `--no-sandbox` on a test-only browser. |
| **Replace Karma with Playwright/Vitest for unit tests** | Valid long-term, but far too much scope for this incident. |

---

## Optional hardening (not required for the fix)

1. **Post-create sanity check** — like `ensure-playwright-browsers.sh`, add a one-liner that launches Chromium with `--no-sandbox` so sandbox misconfig fails at provision time, not mid-`precommit-check`.
2. **Conditional flags** — only apply `--no-sandbox` when `CI=1` or a devcontainer env var is set. Unnecessary if everyone runs tests in DevPod; unconditional flags are fine on macOS/Linux dev machines too.

---

## Bottom line

**Fix in rebase-placeholder:** custom Karma launcher + `--no-sandbox` (and the two companion flags). Leave DOOD as-is; don’t touch DInD or the embed image for this.

I’m in Ask mode, so I can’t apply the change. Switch to Agent mode if you want it implemented in the repo.

[REDACTED]
